content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob are playing a game.

First, Alice throws NNN darts at a board with dimensions 109×10910^9 \times 10^9109×109 at random. Each dart hits the board at a point (some of these points may coincide). Then, Bob should answer Alice's QQQ questions. In each question, Alice chooses two integers lll and rrr; Bob should only consider all points hit in throws lll through rrr (inclusive) and compute the distance between the farthest pair of these points.

Help Bob find the answers to all queries.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN denoting the number of points.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers xix_ixi​ and yiy_iyi​ denoting the coordinates of the point hit with the iii-th dart.
The next line contains a single integer QQQ denoting the number of queries.
Each of the following QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers lll and rrr describing one query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer — the square of the maximum distance. (It is guaranteed that this number is an integer.)
